nodes.title: 🅛🅣🅧 LTXV Spatio-Temporal Tiled VAE Decode: Parameter Guide
widgets_values:
1. spatial_tiles (int, max 8)

    Description: Determines how many sections the image is split into (H x W). A value of 4 splits the frame into a 4x4 grid (16 tiles).

    Recommendation for 12GB VRAM: Use 4 for 720p. Use 6 or 8 for 1080p to prevent "Out of Memory" (OOM) errors.

    Note: Higher values reduce VRAM usage per step, which is essential for the RTX 5080's 12GB limit.

2. spatial_overlap (int, max 8)

    Description: The overlap between tiles in "Latent Units" (1 unit = 32 pixels). An overlap of 4 equals 128 pixels.

    Recommendation: Set to 4 by default. If you see visible grid lines or "seams" in your video, increase this to the maximum of 8.

3. temporal_tile_length (int, max 1000)

    Description: Defines how many video frames the VAE processes simultaneously in a single batch.

    Recommendation for 12GB VRAM: Stick to 16 or 24 for 1080p stability. While the limit is 1000, 12GB cards will crash (OOM) if this value is set too high.

4. temporal_overlap (int, max 8)

    Description: The amount of overlap between different time segments. This ensures smooth motion continuity between frame batches.

    Recommendation: Set to 4. For very fast-moving subjects, use 8 to prevent stuttering between segments.

5. last-frame-fix (bool)

    Description: Addresses a known LTX-VAE bug where the final frame of a clip often contains noise, artifacts, or turns black.
